intentional_test_features: Valvular heart failure: progressive exertional dyspnoea and fatigue with new orthopnoea and nocturnal cough, acutely worse over two weeks, on a background of chronic severe mitral regurgitation now decompensating into pulmonary congestion

Onset-of-symptoms trigger: symptom onset in chronic severe MR is a marker that the ventricle is failing and surgery is usually needed - the AI Doctor should recognise this as a decompensation, not a minor grumble
Resting hypoxaemia/tachypnoea (SpO2 94%, RR20) with orthopnoea indicates early pulmonary oedema needing urgent assessment and diuresis

Structural clues are sealed: the diagnosis rests on a clinician-auscultated apical pansystolic murmur radiating to the axilla, displaced hyperdynamic apex, soft S1 and an S3 - none obtainable over telehealth

Telehealth limits: safe diuresis (with renal/electrolyte monitoring) and confirmatory echocardiography require in-person assessment, so the correct action is urgent same-day assessment, not a remote fluid tablet or next-day wait

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
